summary: Classified the inventory shard as two independent lanes: #89855 is already merged and closed for the Telegram DM context issue, while #95113 remains the open memory-core canonical repair path. No merge is recommended because #95113 lacks a fresh complete merge preflight and has one failed check; a narrow in-place repair artifact is planned for the editable contributor branch. #83633 is routed to central security because the hydrated artifact marks it security-sensitive. #92891 is retained only as non-mutating historical evidence because it carries a blocked merge-risk label and is not executable repair lineage.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair the editable #95113 contributor branch in place so the memory-core shadow database sidecar cleanup fix can reach merge gates. The intended repair is narrow: preserve the contributor's behavior change, inspect the failed checks-node-compact-small-7 lane for branch-caused failures, adjust only the memory cleanup implementation or focused regression tests if needed, then rerun changed validation and require a fresh clean Codex review before any merge recommendation.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(memory): keep cleaning reindex sidecars after lock errors
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves
Refs #78640 and repairs #95113 in place. Current main can stop direct memory reindex sidecar cleanup after the first filesystem deletion error, leaving later `.memory-reindex-*` SQLite sidecars behind.

## Why This Change Was Made
#94646 removed the old full-file rename path, but the current SQLite shadow cleanup path still needs to keep attempting all owned sidecars before surfacing the first cleanup failure. #95113 is the narrow contributor PR for that behavior and is editable by maintainers, so the safest path is to repair that branch rather than replace it.

## User Impact
Memory reindex cleanup is more robust after lock or filesystem errors, especially on Windows-style failure modes where one sidecar deletion can fail while later sidecars are still removable.

## Evidence
- Current main `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.ts` aborts direct `removeMemoryDatabaseFiles` cleanup on an uncaught `fs.rmSync` error.
- #95113 is a two-file memory-core patch with proof supplied, but the hydrated artifact shows one failed `checks-node-compact-small-7` check and no fresh complete merge preflight.
- Validation plan: `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts`, then `pnpm check:changed`; deterministic merge tooling must also run Codex `/review` and exact-head gates before merge.
credit_notes: Repair #95113 in place; preserve contributor @849261680 as the source author for the memory-core sidecar cleanup fix.
If a follow-up commit is needed, keep the PR lineage and PR body attribution tied to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95113.

fix_actions.reason: job blocks force_push required to update rebased contributor branch fix/78640-memory-reindex-windows-fallback
